channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Tony Award winners list: 'Schmigadoon!' wins best musical, 'Death of a Salesman' lives on
item.description: The 79th Annual Tony Awards celebrated the best of Broadway performances on Sunday in New York, but the star of the night was singer-songwriter P!nk, who hosted the show.
item.content:encoded:
The 79th Annual Tony Awards celebrated the best of Broadway performances on Sunday in New York, but the star of the night was singer-songwriter P!nk, who hosted the show.

(Image credit: Charles Sykes)
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Vanasco

item.title: It's cleaning season for the Art Institute of Chicago's miniature rooms
item.description: At the Art Institute of Chicago, cleaning the Thorne Rooms, shoebox-sized rooms presenting a visual history of interior design in miniature, is a meticulous and delicate process.
item.content:encoded: At the Art Institute of Chicago, cleaning the Thorne Rooms, shoebox-sized rooms presenting a visual history of interior design in miniature, is a meticulous and delicate process.
item.dc:creator: Minju Park

item.title: Sunday Puzzle: NBA Team Names
item.description: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe plays the puzzle with OPB listener Jocelyn Tutak of Portland, OR., along with Weekend Edition Puzzlemaster Will Shortz.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe plays the puzzle with OPB listener Jocelyn Tutak of Portland, OR., along with Weekend Edition Puzzlemaster Will Shortz.
item.dc:creator: Will Shortz

item.title: It's not just T-shirts — how Broadway merchandise is changing
item.description: Every year, we highlight essential Broadway workers not eligible for Tonys. This year, we look at the world of Broadway merch, which is a lot more than T-shirts, mugs and refrigerator magnets.
item.content:encoded:
Every year, we highlight essential Broadway workers not eligible for Tonys. This year, we look at the world of Broadway merch, which is a lot more than T-shirts, mugs and refrigerator magnets.

(Image credit: Matthew Murphy and Evan Zimmerman)
item.dc:creator: Jeff Lunden

item.title: Summer TV season has arrived — here's what you shouldn't miss
item.description: This summer brings remakes of Cape Fear and Little House on the Prairie, plus brand new seasons of The Bear, Ted Lasso, House of the Dragon and Star Trek: Strange New Worlds.
item.content:encoded:
'/>

This summer brings remakes of Cape Fear and Little House on the Prairie, plus brand new seasons of The Bear, Ted Lasso, House of the Dragon and Star Trek: Strange New Worlds.
item.dc:creator: Eric Deggans

item.title: 'Wait Wait' for June 6. 2026: Live in Austin with Not My Job guest Elana Meyers Taylor
item.description: This week, Wait Wait is live in Austin with host Peter Sagal, special guest Elana Meyers Taylor and panelists Brian Babylon, Rachel Coster, and Tom Papa
item.content:encoded:
This week, Wait Wait is live in Austin with host Peter Sagal, special guest Elana Meyers Taylor and panelists Brian Babylon, Rachel Coster, and Tom Papa

(Image credit: Julian Finney)

item.title: In Ohio, the Sikh community passes traditions to the next generation
item.description: Photographer Akash Pamarthy has documented the Sikh religious community in Ohio over several years. His photos tell a story.
item.content:encoded:
Photographer Akash Pamarthy has documented the Sikh religious community in Ohio over several years. His photos tell a story.

(Image credit: Akash Pamarthy)
item.dc:creator: Akash Pamarthy

item.title: After D.C.'s Reflecting Pool gets repainted, visitors ask: What changed?
item.description: The Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool is refilling after President Trump had it painted "American flag blue." Some visitors say the results of the project — which reportedly cost millions — are subtle.
item.content:encoded:
The Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool is refilling after President Trump had it painted "American flag blue." Some visitors say the results of the project — which reportedly cost millions — are subtle.

(Image credit: Rahmat Gul)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Nick Jonas steals Paul Rudd's 'Power Ballad' in a profound story about art and honesty
item.description: In 'Power Ballad,' a wedding singer played by Paul Rudd writes a hit — and a popstar makes it his.
item.content:encoded:
In 'Power Ballad,' a wedding singer played by Paul Rudd writes a hit — and a popstar makes it his.

(Image credit: David Cleary)
item.dc:creator: Linda Holmes

item.title: 2 young directors strike big at the box office with 'Backrooms' and 'Obsession'
item.description: Backrooms, by 20-year-old filmmaker Kane Parsons, is set in a mysterious maze of abandoned offices. Curry Barker, 26, tells a horror story about consent and male loneliness in Obsession.
item.content:encoded: Backrooms, by 20-year-old filmmaker Kane Parsons, is set in a mysterious maze of abandoned offices. Curry Barker, 26, tells a horror story about consent and male loneliness in Obsession.
item.dc:creator: Justin Chang
